questions.reason: Reason (R): This is because the square of a real number is always non-negative.
questions.assertion: Assertion (A): For any real numbers a and b, the inequality a < b implies a^2 < b^2.
questions.explanation: Since a < b, a^2 - b^2 < 0. Factoring on the left hand side, we get (a + b)(a - b) < 0. Since a + b and a - b have opposite signs, one of them must be negative. Hence, a^2 < b^2.

questions.reason: Reason (R): This is because a linear inequality in one variable has either no solution, exactly one solution, or infinitely many solutions.
questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The inequality 3x - 2 > 5 has exactly one solution.
questions.explanation: The inequality 3x - 2 > 5 is equivalent to 3x > 7, which is equivalent to x > 7/3. This is a linear inequality in one variable and has infinitely many solutions, not exactly one solution.

questions.reason: Reason (R): This is because a quadratic inequality in one variable has either no solution, exactly one solution, or infinitely many solutions.
questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The inequality x^2 - 5 > 0 has exactly one solution.
questions.explanation: The inequality x^2 - 5 > 0 is equivalent to x^2 > 5, which is equivalent to x > sqrt(5) or x < -sqrt(5). This is a quadratic inequality in one variable and has infinitely many solutions, not exactly one solution.
